title: Executor
description: A person appointed to administer an estate. Includes professional executors, administrators (intestacy), and administrators with will annexed.

id.description: Unique identifier for this executor appointment within the INHERIT document.

personId.description: Reference to the Person.id of the appointed executor or administrator.

role.$comment: primary: the first-named executor who acts unless unable or unwilling. secondary: acts alongside the primary executor or takes over if the primary cannot act. substitute: named as a replacement if the primary and secondary cannot act. administrator: appointed by the court when there is no will (intestacy). administrator_with_will_annexed: appointed by the court when the will exists but the named executor cannot or will not act.
role.description: The executor's role in the estate administration.

isProfessional.description: Whether this executor is a professional (solicitor, accountant, trust corporation). Professional executors typically charge fees.

firmName.description: Name of the professional firm, if the executor is acting in a professional capacity.

grantType.$comment: grant_of_probate: court confirms the executor named in the will. letters_of_administration: court appoints an administrator when there is no will. letters_of_administration_with_will_annexed: court appoints an administrator when the named executor cannot act. resealing: a foreign grant is recognised by a local court (common in Commonwealth jurisdictions). european_certificate_of_succession: EU document proving an heir's or executor's status under the Brussels IV Regulation. Without a grant, the executor has no legal authority to administer the estate.
grantType.description: The type of grant issued by the court authorising this person to act.

grantReference.description: Court reference number for the grant.

issuingCourt.description: Name of the court or registry that issued the grant.

notes.description: Free-text notes about this executor appointment — reservations, renunciations, or special circumstances.
examples: Power reserved to the secondary executor
Executor renounced — administrator with will annexed appointed
